failures.explanation: Ratio-on-ratio framework limit. CCoAOMT1 methylates caffeoyl-CoA upstream of the G/S branch point. Biologically KD reduces both G and S proportionally leaving S/G unchanged (Zhong 2000). The scalar Lignin_SG_Ratio node cannot represent 'both branches reduce proportionally' because there is no separate Lignin_G and Lignin_S node to cancel.
failures.fix_strategy: Would require splitting Lignin_SG_Ratio into Lignin_G and Lignin_S sub-nodes. Out of scope of surgical refinement.

failures.explanation: Ratio-on-ratio framework limit. CAD1 reduces both coniferaldehyde and sinapaldehyde with similar kinetics; biologically KD does not shift S/G (Baucher 1996). The network wires CAD1 to both Sinapyl_Alcohol (+1 to SG) and Coniferyl_Alcohol (-1 to SG), but geometric-mean activation and bounded-inverse inhibition have asymmetric magnitudes — Sinapyl_Alcohol drop dominates and SG falls. Cannot be fixed without removing CAD1 from both alcohol edges, which would break COMT1 propagation (T005/T006).
failures.fix_strategy: Would require separating Sinapyl / Coniferyl alcohol contributions via a sub-node split. Retaining CAD1 as-is is the honest model compromise.

failures.explanation: Genetic-background heterogeneity. T011 (Coleman 2008 PNAS) and T012 (Ralph 2012) in P. alba x grandidentata show C3H KD increases S/G. T013 (Peng 2021) in P. alba x glandulosa shows the opposite. The network encodes the Coleman/Ralph consensus (C3H1 -> Lignin_SG_Ratio sign=-1) which gets T011, T012 right and T013 wrong. No single sign-coded edge can simultaneously satisfy both published directions.
failures.fix_strategy: Not addressable without a background-stratified model. Acceptable residual at 2/3 C3H_RNAi.

failures.explanation: Non-monotonic biology. Ahlawat 2021 reports BOTH KNAT7 OE (T026, +5-7% S/G) AND KNAT7 KD (T027, +8-12% S/G) produce S/G increases, with total-lignin drop only in the KD case. A sign-encoded cascade (where +1 means 'more regulator = more output') cannot represent bell-shaped responses where both too-much and too-little of the regulator shift composition in the same direction. The model gets T026 right and T027 wrong.
failures.fix_strategy: Would require non-monotonic response functions not part of the FLASH-P framework.

summary.note_iter1_fix: Iteration 1 removed T016 (4CL5 CRISPR KO) from the failure list by removing the PT4CL5 node per Wang 2020. T016 is now correctly skipped as 'not in network' rather than misclassified.
summary.note_algebraic_vs_ode: Algebraic method still produces 13 failures vs 4 in ODE. The additional 9 algebraic-only failures (T008, T009, T017, T018, T023, T024, T025, T026, T029) are all cases where the propagated ratio sits within +/- 0.05 of the threshold because geometric-mean activation dilutes signals through a 2-3 step cascade. Hill-function dynamics (K=2, n=1) amplify these signals and recover the correct direction. These are NOT structural network issues.
